page: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an Intel Broadwell x86_64 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. It features 6 shared vCPUs with 6 physical cores and 1 thread per core, backed by 64.0 GB of system memory, which provides 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Graphics acceleration is delivered by a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1745.38, while the multi-core score reaches 10619.81. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 586.08 GB/sec, dropping to 76.25 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 43.9 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the weak tier at 30.39 GB/sec, whereas redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 10.78 million operations per second.

This server offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads that require moderate compute capabilities but benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated VRAM. The combination of 64.0 GB of system RAM, 16 GB of VRAM, and local NVMe storage makes it suitable for workloads such as virtual desktop infrastructure hosting, media streaming, entry-level machine learning inference, and database operations.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server designed for workloads requiring dedicated graphics acceleration and a high memory footprint. It features 6 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a 350 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 10619.81 and a redis SET throughput of 10.78 million operations per second. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense tasks, making it suitable for virtual desktop infrastructure, media transcoding, and database operations.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-6c-64g-16vram is a Cloud GPU server with 6 Intel vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, 350 GB NVMe SSD, and one NVIDIA A16 GPU with 16 GB VRAM.
tagline: An accelerated cloud server featuring dedicated NVIDIA Ampere graphics, high memory capacity, and fast local NVMe storage for virtualized workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1x NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU featuring 16 GB of VRAM
Configured with 6 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Includes 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast data access
Delivers average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 10619.81
Suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, media streaming, and databases
